title: Accessing Outer Class Members
hints: In Java, a non-static nested class (also known as an inner class) has a special relationship with its outer class. Specifically, it can access all members (fields and methods) of the outer class, including those declared as private.
description: Given an outer class Outer with a private integer field x, implement a method calculate(int factor) inside a non-static inner class Inner that returns the product of x and the given factor.

examples.explanation: The inner class accesses the private field x (10) and multiplies it by the factor (5). Result: 10 * 5 = 50.
Accessing negative values works exactly the same way. -2 * 10 = -20.

constraints: The outer class must contain a private field x.
The inner class must be non-static.

title: Static Nested Classes
hints: Static nested classes do not have access to non-static members of the outer class.
description: Implement a static nested class that provides a utility function for the outer class.

examples.explanation: Static method square returns the square of the input.

constraints: The nested class must be static.
The method must be accessible without an instance of the outer class.
